original: First batsman to ever score 1000 runs in a single innings in cricket - 15 year-old Mumbai schoolboy Pranav Dhanawade is 1009 not out
An Alaskan Airlines Boeing 737 Max 9 makes emergency landing in Portland after a fuselage section blows out midair, with US regulator then grounding hundreds of planes
Chinese professor Zhang Yongzhen publishes the first SARS-CoV-2 genome map online, allowing health professionals worldwide to identify COVID-19

Eris, the largest known dwarf planet in the solar system, is discovered by the team of Michael E. Brown, Chad Trujillo, and David L. Rabinowitz using images originally taken on October 21, 2003, at the Palomar Observatory.
Kathrine Switzer, American marathon runner, first woman to run the Boston Marathon in 1967, born in Amberg, Germany[1]
Shah Jahan, 5th Mughal Emperor of India (1628-58) who built the Taj Mahal for his beloved wife Mumtaz Mahal, born in Lahore, Mughal Empire (d. 1666)
"Die Presse" newspaper (Germany) publicly announcesWilhelm Röntgen's discovery of X-rays and their potential for new methods of medical diagnoses in a front-page article
Kiowa Indians record this as the night the stars fell
Globetrotters lose 100-99 to NJ Reds, ending 2,495-game winning streak
Kevin Bradshaw of US Intl scores NCAA Div 1 record 72 pts
Alvin Ailey, African-American dancer and choreographer (American Dance Theater), born in Rogers, Texas (d. 1989)

"Bozo the Clown" live children's show premieres on TV
USSR Venera 5 launches for 1st successful planet landing (Venus)
Discovery moves to launch pad for STS 51-C mission

"Strange Case of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde" byRobert Louis Stevensonpublished by Longmans, Green & Co.
Elizabeth Cotten, American folk and blues guitarist and songwriter ("Freight Train", Shake Sugaree"), born in Carrboro, North Carolina (d. 1987)

Work on Golden Gate Bridge begins, starting on the Marin County side
IndustrialistHenry Fordannounces his $5 minimum per-day wage, doubling most workers pay from $2.40 for a 9hr day to $5 for an 8hr day

Brian Larascores his maiden Test century with 277 for the West Indies v Australia in drawn 3rd Test at the Sydney Cricket Ground
Carolyn Schuler, American swimmer (Olympic gold 100m butterfly, 4x100m medley relay WR 4:41.1 1960), born in San Francisco, California (d. 2024)
The Great Frost begins during the night, a sudden cold snap that remains Europe's coldest ever winter. Thousands are killed across the continent and crops fail in France.

Davy Crockettarrives in Nacogdoches, Texas, to aid the revolution
Salyut 4 with crew of 2 is launched for 30 days

Boston's Fenway Park catches fire for 2nd time (May 8th 1926 also)
Alfred Brendel, Austrian pianist (first to record Beethoven's complete works for solo piano), musicologist, and poet, born in Loučná nad Desnou, Czechoslovakia (d. 2025)[1]
Incorporation of the National Association of Audubon Society, a nonprofit environmental organization dedicated to conservation
Charles Perrine announces discovery of Jupiter's 7th satellite, Elara
FCC hears the 1st transmission of FM radio with clear, static-free signal
Operation Moses, covert airlifting of about 8000 Ethiopian Jews from war-torn Sudan to Israel via Belgium ends
Nigerian film "A Tribe Called Judah", created by Funke Akindele, becomes the first Nollywood film to earn more than $1bn naira ($1.1m) domestically
Jeannette Piccard, American high-altitude balloonist & Episcopal priest, born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 1981)
Guru Gobind Singh, Tenth Guru of Sikhism, founder and General of Khalsa Army, social reformist and poet, born in Patna Sahib (d. 1708)
Chen Kenichi [Ken'ichi Azuma], Japanese celebrity chef, "The Szechuan Sage" (Iron Chef Chinese), born in Tokyo, Japan
Elizabeth Mrema, Tanzanian environmental leader (UN Environment Program), born in Kilimanjaro Region, Tanzania
Stephen Cole Kleene, American mathematician (Regular Expressions), born in Hartford, Connecticut (d. 1994)

-42°F (-41.1°C), Smethport, Pennsylvania (state record)
